en.name: C reactive protein (CRP) in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context C reactive protein (CRP) is an acute-phase protein produced by the liver in response to inflammation, infection or tissue injury. Plasma CRP rises within 6-12 hours of an inflammatory stimulus, peaks around 48 hours, and falls quickly once the stimulus resolves [1]. It is one of the most widely used biomarkers of inflammation in intensive care, infectious diseases and rheumatology. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser by immunoassay. Results are reported as a mass concentration in mg/L (SI / European convention) or mg/dL (US convention), or less commonly as a substance concentration in nmol/L [2]. **Note: standard CRP vs high sensitivity CRP (hsCRP).** The two are measured on the same analyte (CRP) but with different assay sensitivities. Standard CRP assays have a lower limit of detection around 3-5 mg/L, sufficient for monitoring active infection or inflammation (typical values 10-300 mg/L). High sensitivity CRP (hsCRP) assays can quantify down to ~ 0.1 mg/L and are used to detect low-grade chronic inflammation, especially for cardiovascular risk stratification [3]. Both forms are included in this concept set; downstream analyses that distinguish them must use the LOINC code, not the value. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on four LOINC Hierarchy concepts (scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), each expanded to include all their descendants. The set resolves to nine LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Serum or plasma - *3020460 / 1988-5 — C reactive protein [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used. - *3010156 / 30522-7 — C reactive protein [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by High sensitivity method (LOINC)*: hsCRP. - *46234770 / 76485-2 — C reactive protein [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: SI form. - *46234771 / 76486-0 — C reactive protein [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma by High sensitivity method (LOINC)*: SI form, hsCRP. ### Whole blood - *42870365 / 71426-1 — C reactive protein [Mass/volume] in Blood by High sensitivity method (LOINC)* - *1091617 / 105126-7 — C reactive protein [Mass/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood (LOINC)* - *1469985 / 106736-2 — C reactive protein [Mass/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood by Rapid immunoassay (LOINC)* ### Capillary blood - *3051387 / 48421-2 — C reactive protein [Mass/volume] in Capillary blood (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mg/L`** (concept 8985 — milligram per litre) for `[Mass/volume]` codes and **`nmol/L`** (concept 9573 — nanomole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes. Conversion: `1 mg/L ≈ 9.52 nmol/L` (molar mass of CRP pentamer = 105 kDa, but conversion practiced on the monomer mass of ~ 23 kDa — both rare in practice, mg/L dominates). ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts in Serum or Plasma are deliberately excluded by name: - *3000965 / 11039-5 — C reactive protein [Presence] in Serum or Plasma*: qualitative, not a concentration. - *3034254 / 35648-5 — C reactive protein [Quintile] in Serum or Plasma by High sensitivity method*: categorical (quintile bin), not a concentration. - *3028766 / 14634-0 — C reactive protein [Titer] in Serum or Plasma*: dilution titre, not a concentration. In addition, the set deliberately omits: - **Body fluid, CSF specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Cord blood and fetus specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a CRP value without specifying assay sensitivity: `3020460 / 1988-5 — C reactive protein [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. The most common LOINC code. - **Source explicitly says "hsCRP" or "high sensitivity"**: map to the matching `by High sensitivity method` LOINC code. - **Source unit is mg/dL**: convert to mg/L by multiplying by 10. - **Source reports a qualitative result (positive/negative, titre)**: do **not** map. Qualitative results are out of scope. - **Source reports CRP from CSF or body fluid**: do **not** map. Out of scope. - **Do not confuse with**: procalcitonin (PCT, separate concept set), interleukin-6 (IL-6, separate concept set), erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR, separate concept set if defined). ## References [1] Pepys MB, Hirschfield GM. C-reactive protein: a critical update. *J Clin Invest*. 2003;111(12):1805-12. Available: https://doi.org/10.1172/JCI18921 [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 1988-5 — C reactive protein [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/1988-5/ [3] Ridker PM. A test in context: high-sensitivity C-reactive protein. *J Am Coll Cardiol*. 2016;67(6):712-23. Available: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jacc.2015.11.037
